reasoning: The document is a map-based report where the map is the primary informative element, but the conversion removed the map and replaced it with a text block of county names. While programmatic signals show high text recall (0.975), the visual fidelity score is very low (0.5) because the spatial data and graphics essential to the document's purpose were lost or fundamentally rearranged.

concerns.analysis: The visual organization of the document has been fundamentally altered. The source PDF is a single-page map-based report where the map is the central feature, with the legend and logo positioned relative to it. In the HTML, the map graphic is removed and replaced by a dense block of text containing the county names. The logo, which was a small element in the bottom right of the PDF, has been enlarged and moved to a central position. The legend has been converted into a standard vertical list bel
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A central map of North Carolina counties with status coloring, with a legend in the bottom-left and a logo in the bottom-right.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML displays the title, followed by a large logo, then a paragraph of county names ('HYDE DARE CART...'), and then a bulleted list for the legend.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains two meaningful graphics that are missing in the HTML: the colored map of North Carolina counties and the north arrow icon (top left). While the logo has been preserved (albeit resized and split), the map is the primary informative element of the document and its absence as an image is a major defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Colored map of North Carolina counties and a north arrow icon.
concerns.output_quote: The map graphic and north arrow are missing; only text labels from the map are present.
